refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral and specifies what the system must achieve without prescribing how. There are no multiple obligations requiring decomposition.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets
Use a redundant communication path strategy to ensure packet delivery meets the loss criteria
Incorporate a buffering and smoothing mechanism at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on monitoring and control of network quality parameters independently from other system functions, supporting modularity and minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that network conditions can be monitored and controlled dynamically within the system scope
Risk that network variability outside system control may cause packet loss beyond target
Assumes that operational policies allow for dynamic adjustment of communication parameters
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including mechanisms for handling network conditions outside system control, such as fallback modes or alerts, to improve robustness.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses network-related functionalities including monitoring and control of network parameters to ensure quality of service.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network performance data and send monitoring reports.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to adjust transmission parameters and communicate with network hardware.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as network quality monitoring and control logically belong here; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module is focused on vehicle control and dynamics; network control would be peripheral and cause high diffusion.
Teleoperation depends on network quality but does not own network control; moderate diffusion expected.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts and monitoring, not network control; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the network quality monitoring and control DP, as it directly manages network parameters and quality assurance.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry interface may require coordination with Teleoperation module.
Adjusting transmission parameters might impact Vehicle or Teleoperation modules indirectly, requiring careful interface management.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet the packet loss target, directly supporting teleoperation communication quality requirements.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry interface may require coordination with Teleoperation module.
Adjusting transmission parameters might impact Vehicle or Teleoperation modules indirectly, requiring careful interface management.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within this IM boundary. Other modules show poor containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and validating that packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) supporting the required functionality.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface coordination protocols between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to manage dependencies and minimize diffusion risks.
Include impact analysis procedures for transmission parameter adjustments on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to ensure system stability and safety compliance.

decision.justification: Although the current FR, DP, and IM mappings pass all gates with strong justification and containment, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks related to interface dependencies and impact on other modules necessitate further decomposition. Deriving next-level sub-FRs will enable more detailed specification and control of these interfaces and impact analyses, improving safety assurance and risk mitigation.
next_level_sub_frs: Monitor network packet loss to detect deviations from target
Control transmission parameters dynamically to maintain packet loss within limits
Coordinate interface protocols between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules
Analyze impact of transmission parameter adjustments on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

revise.why: To explicitly incorporate interface coordination and impact analysis requirements into the functional decomposition, ensuring these critical aspects are addressed at the functional level.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module encapsulates the DP effectively with minimal diffusion.
Telemetry and Network Link interfaces provide sufficient observability and control for verification.
Interface coordination protocols and impact analysis procedures can be defined and verified at sub-FR level.
open_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry interface requiring coordination with Teleoperation module.
Adjusting transmission parameters might impact Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, risking system stability.
Verification feasibility depends on accurate monitoring and validation of packet loss metrics under varying network conditions.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface coordination protocols between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
Impact analysis procedures and safety assessments for transmission parameter adjustments.
Verification test plans demonstrating packet loss control under representative network conditions.
